authors: Thomson SJ, Tavakkolizadeh M, Love-Jones S, Patel NK, Gu JW, Bains A, Doan Q, Moffitt M
title: Effects of Rate on Analgesia in Kilohertz Frequency Spinal Cord Stimulation: Results of the PROCO Randomized Controlled Trial
journal_name: Neuromodulation: Technology at the Neural Interface
study_question: Investigate effects of kilohertz frequency (1-10 kHz) spinal cord stimulation rate on analgesia.
population_assessed: 20 patients with persistent low back pain, with or without leg pain, implanted with SCS.
follow_up_duration: Four weeks per frequency, followed by three months post-randomization.
outcome_measures: ED-NRS pain scores (back, leg, overall), ODI, EQ-5D-5L, PSQI, PGIC.
inclusion_criteria: Persistent low back pain ≥ 90 days, mean pain intensity ≥ 5, stable opioid medication.
answer_to_study_question: 1-10 kHz SCS frequencies provide equivalent pain relief; 1 kHz requires significantly less charge.
